canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, D4RL (Datasets for Deep Data-Driven Reinforcement Learning) is defined as a benchmark suite for offline reinforcement learning. Its application is noted in the context of risk-sensitive tasks, with one study conducting experiments on "four risk-sensitive D4RL tasks" involving human preferences. The suite is also explicitly used as a measurement instrument for the construct of Decision-making.
papers: A Distributional Approach to Uncertainty-Aware Preference Alignment Using Offline Demonstrations
Zero-shot Model-based Reinforcement Learning using Large Language Models

definitions.text: Datasets for Deep Data-Driven Reinforcement Learning (D4RL), a benchmark suite for offline reinforcement learning, used here in risk-sensitive variants.
definitions.paper: A Distributional Approach to Uncertainty-Aware Preference Alignment Using Offline Demonstrations
